title: Conagra Brands · Capstone Charter
subtitle: Forecasting & analytics for a Fortune 500 CPG company
highlights: Currently leading an MIT MBAn capstone project with Conagra Brands
Building forecasting and analytics tooling to support demand and product planning decisions
Translating ambiguous business questions into a reproducible data-science workflow
description: Capstone team project with Conagra Brands as part of the MIT Master of Business Analytics program. The work is currently in progress — high-level focus is using machine learning and time-series methods to support forecasting and decision-making for a Fortune 500 consumer packaged goods company. Project deliverables and detailed materials are confidential under the program's industry charter, so I'm sharing only a high-level summary here.

title: Optimization · Water Access in the Democratic Republic of Congo
subtitle: Prescriptive optimization for high-impact well placement
highlights: Built scalable optimization pipelines in Julia / JuMP
Identified high-impact well locations across the DRC
Improved projected water access by 19%
description: MIT Sloan Optimization Methods project applying mixed-integer programming to humanitarian infrastructure planning. The model selects new well locations across the Democratic Republic of Congo to maximize population reached and minimize travel distance to clean water, subject to budget and geographic constraints. Pipelines were built to scale across very large candidate-site sets and to compare multiple policy scenarios.

title: Predictive + Prescriptive Solar Battery Strategy
subtitle: ML forecasts feeding an optimization model for grid-scale storage
highlights: Forecasted next-day solar generation and electricity market prices
Built a JuMP optimization that prescribes when to charge, hold, or sell
End-to-end predictive → prescriptive pipeline for Southern California
description: MIT Sloan Machine Learning project that combines predictive modeling and prescriptive optimization to operate a grid-scale battery in Southern California. The predictive layer forecasts next-day solar generation and market clearing prices; the prescriptive layer is a JuMP optimization that decides charging, holding, and selling actions to maximize revenue subject to physical battery constraints. The full pipeline demonstrates how ML and optimization can be cleanly chained together.

title: Boston Public Health Commission · Shelter Occupancy Forecasting
subtitle: ML models for capacity planning at city homeless shelters
highlights: Forecasted 14-day shelter occupancy across Boston shelters
Improved capacity prediction accuracy by 50% vs. baseline
Identified key social, environmental, and temporal predictors of utilization
Presented interpretable insights to BPHC program managers and city partners
description: MIT Sloan Analytics Lab project with the Boston Public Health Commission. The team built and deployed a suite of machine-learning models — including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and ARIMA — to forecast 14-day occupancy across the city's homeless shelter network. Feature-importance analysis surfaced the social, environmental, and temporal factors driving demand, giving BPHC actionable signal for capacity planning and overflow mitigation.

title: Greater Boston Data Visualization · Proof of Concept
subtitle: Interactive visual exploration of Greater Boston
highlights: Interactive proof-of-concept visualization of Greater Boston
Live deployment hosted on GitHub Pages
Collaborated as part of an MIT MBAn data-visualization team
description: Collaborative MIT data-visualization project exploring Greater Boston through an interactive proof-of-concept. The project showcases how careful chart design and geospatial layering can make a complex regional dataset legible and explorable. View the live site for the full experience.

title: Optimization · F1 Race Calendar Redesign
subtitle: Cutting Formula 1 logistics emissions with combinatorial optimization
highlights: Modeled the Formula 1 calendar as a routing/sequencing optimization in AMPL
Re-sequenced races to minimize global travel distance under FIA constraints
Estimated a reduction of 927 tons of CO₂ per team
description: UC Berkeley Nonlinear & Discrete Optimization project that re-imagines the Formula 1 calendar as a constrained routing problem. The AMPL model finds a re-ordering of races that minimizes global travel distance while respecting FIA scheduling and seasonal constraints. The redesigned calendar is estimated to save roughly 927 tons of CO₂ per team per season.

title: Voltage Discharge Prediction · Green Hydrogen Electrolyzers
subtitle: Sensor-data ML for material performance under intermittent renewables
highlights: Cleaned and modeled multivariate sensor data from 10 lab-scale electrolyzers
Predicted voltage discharge curves under intermittent renewable input
Reduced data cleaning labor by 80% through automated noise/outlier handling
description: Industry research at Verdagy on green hydrogen electrolyzer performance. Built Python pipelines to ingest, denoise, and align multivariate sensor streams from 10 lab-scale electrolyzers, then trained models to predict voltage discharge curves under simulated renewable-energy intermittency. Insights informed material durability decisions for next-generation cells.

title: Yelp Sentiment Analysis
subtitle: NLP on millions of reviews to quantify customer satisfaction
highlights: Tokenized and modeled millions of Yelp reviews
Quantified sentiment patterns across business categories
Surfaced category-level satisfaction signal for downstream analysis
description: UC Berkeley NLP project applying text preprocessing, tokenization, and sentiment classification to the public Yelp Open Dataset. The work measures customer satisfaction at the business and category level and explores the linguistic features most predictive of star ratings.

title: MongoDB vs SQL · Query Performance Analysis
subtitle: Benchmarking relational vs. document stores on the Yelp dataset
highlights: Benchmarked equivalent analytical queries across MongoDB and SQL
Compared query speed, scalability, and developer ergonomics
Wrote up recommendations for when to choose each store
description: Data-engineering project that puts MongoDB and a relational SQL store head-to-head on the Yelp dataset. Equivalent analytical queries were authored in both systems and benchmarked across speed, scalability, and ease of expression to inform when each technology is the right tool.

title: Marketing Analytics · Kaggle Competition (Top 10)
subtitle: Product-analytics modeling that placed in the top 10 on the leaderboard
highlights: Top-10 leaderboard finish in a class-wide marketing-analytics Kaggle competition
Heavy feature engineering and ensembling on customer behavior data
Demonstrates practical product-analytics modeling end-to-end
description: Kaggle-style marketing analytics competition (Assignment 3) where my team placed in the top 10 on the leaderboard. The project involved heavy feature engineering on customer behavior data and ensembling of gradient-boosted models. It is highlighted here as evidence of practical product-analytics work, even though the underlying notebook is not posted on this site.

title: ArcGIS Mapping · ESPM 110 Environmental Analyses
subtitle: Spatial analyses of environmental and ecological systems
highlights: Produced map deliverables for UC Berkeley's ESPM 110 course
Layered ecological and demographic data for environmental analysis
Practiced classical cartographic design for technical reports
description: Selected map outputs from coursework in UC Berkeley's ESPM 110 (The Politics & Ecology of Environmental Health). The full reports are coursework, but the map deliverables — included on the dedicated Maps page — showcase my hands-on ArcGIS work: data layering, spatial joins, classification, and cartographic design.
